text.title: Tech-Debt Budget for Solos Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Tech-Debt Budget for Solos methodology. Every rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale, plus the universal skip-this-methodology gate.

rule.statement: Debt work MUST consume no more than 20% of cycle capacity; any cycle exceeding the cap MUST flag an explicit policy exception with rationale.
rule.rationale: Past 20% the new-feature pipeline starves; below 20% debt compounds. 20% keeps velocity unblocked while paying down interest.

rule.statement: Debt items MUST live in a single named ledger file (`docs/tech-debt-ledger.md` or equivalent); items scattered in code-comments or issues are rejected from the cap calculation.
rule.rationale: Scattered debt becomes invisible; a single ledger is the only auditable source for cap math.

rule.statement: Each item MUST carry an `impact` score (1-5) and `interest` rate (1-5, how fast it gets worse); unscored items are rejected.
rule.rationale: Without scores there is no priority ordering; LLMs and humans alike default to 'easy first' which leaves load-bearing debt.

rule.statement: Each item MUST carry an explicit `decision` for the current cycle: ship / pay / monitor; defer-without-decision is rejected.
rule.rationale: Implicit defers stack up; explicit decision (even 'monitor') forces accountability.

rule.statement: Once per month the owner MUST sign the ledger (initials + UTC timestamp); >35 days since sign-off auto-flags the ledger as stale.
rule.rationale: Without a cadence the ledger ossifies; signed cadence forces re-evaluation.

rule.statement: Apply this methodology only if every entry in `## Applies If` holds and none of `## Skip If` triggers; otherwise abort and route to the correct methodology.

rationale.source: F-066 spec - decision tree minimum form

rule.rationale: Misrouted methodology produces wrong-shape output; explicit skip rule is the universal default branch.
